mechanism_narrative: Elevated federal funds rate above 4.5% signals restrictive monetary policy that increases discount rates on equity valuations and reduces corporate profitability through higher borrowing costs, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 55% of instances when FEDFUNDS > 4.5%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate below 3.5% indicates tight labor market conditions that signal late-cycle expansion with wage pressure and inflation risk, constraining profit margins and triggering equity market repricing downward over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in < 45% of instances when UNRATE < 3.5%, or correlation between UNRATE and 5-day forward returns is positive and significant (p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month decline in retail sales combined with declining housing starts signals weakening consumer demand and construction activity, reducing corporate revenue expectations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS declines > 1% MoM AND HOUST declines > 5% MoM
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 50% of instances when both RSAFS and HOUST decline simultaneously, or joint interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production index above 103.0 indicates robust manufacturing and supply-side capacity utilization, supporting corporate earnings growth and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days when sustained above this threshold.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in > 50% of instances when INDPRO > 103.0, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment growth combined with stable inflation signals healthy labor market without wage-price spiral risk, supporting equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS growth > 0.1% MoM AND CPIAUCSL growth < 0.3% MoM
regime_hint: goldilocks labor market with stable prices
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in > 55% of instances when both conditions are met, or interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth measured quarterly at levels above 20,200 billion chained dollars indicates sustained economic expansion that supports corporate earnings and equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in > 50% of quarters when GDPC1 > 20,200, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation measured by CPI index above 305 combined with elevated federal funds rate signals persistent monetary tightening that constrains equity valuations through higher discount rates and reduced real earnings growth, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 55% of instances when both conditions are met, or interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts below 1,400 thousand units signal weakening residential construction demand and forward-looking economic weakness, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as markets reprice growth expectations downward.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 60% of instances when HOUST < 1400, or correlation between HOUST and 5-day forward returns is positive and significant (p < 0.05).
